outbound_official.note: No official Egypt government outbound visa-list page located; rely on destination-side data. Exhaustive search conducted 2026-06-14: mfa.gov.eg (Egyptian Ministry of Foreign Affairs) is in beta ("إصدار تجريبي") with no dedicated page listing visa-free/visa-on-arrival/e-visa destinations for Egyptian ordinary passport holders. moi.gov.eg/passports returns access-denied. No equivalent of India's mea.gov.in/vffin exists on any Egyptian .gov.eg domain. Searched: mfa.gov.eg, mofa.gov.eg (connection refused), moi.gov.eg, sis.gov.eg (TLS error), visa2egypt.gov.eg (covers inbound only), egyptembassy.net (covers entry to Egypt only).

visa_free.nationality: United Kingdom nationals visiting Sinai Peninsula resorts only
visa_free.notes: Free entry permission stamp granted upon arrival. Valid ONLY for Sharm El Sheikh, Dahab, Nuweiba and Taba resorts. Travellers intending to go outside these areas MUST obtain a visa. The consulate visa page states 15 days; the consulate FAQ page states 14 days — a discrepancy between two pages of the same source; 15 days is used here per the primary visas page.

visa_free.nationality: EU member state nationals visiting Sinai Peninsula resorts only
visa_free.notes: Free entry permission stamp granted upon arrival. Valid ONLY for Sharm El Sheikh, Dahab, Nuweiba and Taba resorts. Travellers intending to go outside these areas MUST obtain a visa. Applies to all EU member state nationals. Same 14 vs 15-day discrepancy exists between consulate pages; 15 days used per primary visas page.

visa_free.nationality: United States of America nationals visiting Sinai Peninsula resorts only
visa_free.notes: Free entry permission stamp granted upon arrival. Valid ONLY for Sharm El Sheikh, Dahab, Nuweiba and Taba resorts. Travellers intending to go outside these areas MUST obtain a visa.

visa_policy.notes: The official Egypt e-Visa portal (visa2egypt.gov.eg) is operated by the Egyptian Ministry of Interior. As of April 2026, fees are: single entry $30, multiple entry $65 (increased $5 each from prior $25/$60 rates). Single-entry e-visa valid 90 days from issue; multiple-entry valid 180 days; max stay per entry 30 days. Visa on arrival fee also increased to $30 (single) as of 1 March 2026. The Egypt Consulate UK visas page lists nationalities eligible for visa on arrival (UK, EU, USA, Australia, Canada, Japan, South Korea, New Zealand, Norway, Croatia, Georgia, North Macedonia) and also Russia, Serbia, Ukraine per the FAQ page. Free 15-day entry stamp (not a full visa) available at Sinai Peninsula resorts for UK, EU, and USA nationals. Nationals requiring pre-approval before visa issuance: Afghanistan, Ethiopia, Iran, Israel, Mali, Niger, Palestine, Sudan, Syria, and Travel Document holders. Bangladeshi nationals require a visa prior to entry.

visa_types.name: Tourist Sticker Visa
visa_types.notes: Applied at Egyptian embassies/consulates. Single-entry 30 days; multiple-entry available. Extendable inside Egypt at Passport and Immigration offices. Required for nationalities not eligible for visa on arrival or e-Visa.

visa_types.name: Visa on Arrival
visa_types.notes: Available to nationals of approximately 46 countries at Egyptian international airports. USD 25 fee paid on arrival (USD banknotes accepted). Sinai Only permit (free) available for South Sinai resorts for some nationalities. Extendable inside Egypt.

visa_types.name: e-Visa
visa_types.notes: Available to nationals of eligible countries via the official Egypt e-Visa portal (visa.egov.eg). Single or multiple-entry. 30-day stay, 3-month validity. Online payment by credit card. Approved e-Visa presented on arrival at port of entry.

conditional_access.conditions: Traveller must hold a valid US visa that has been used at least once (entered and exited the issuing country). Visa must be unexpired at time of travel to Egypt. Excluded nationalities: Afghanistan, Ethiopia, Iran, Iraq, Israel, Mali, Niger, Sudan, Yemen. Decree effective from 15 December 2020 as announced by Egypt Ministry of Tourism and Antiquities.

Traveller must hold a valid UK visa that has been used at least once (entered and exited the issuing country). Visa must be unexpired at time of travel to Egypt. Excluded nationalities: Afghanistan, Ethiopia, Iran, Iraq, Israel, Mali, Niger, Sudan, Yemen. Decree effective from 15 December 2020 as announced by Egypt Ministry of Tourism and Antiquities.

credential.subtype: any valid and used Schengen visa

conditional_access.conditions: Traveller must hold a valid Schengen area visa that has been used at least once (entered and exited the Schengen area). Visa must be unexpired at time of travel to Egypt. Excluded nationalities: Afghanistan, Ethiopia, Iran, Iraq, Israel, Mali, Niger, Sudan, Yemen. Decree effective from 15 December 2020 as announced by Egypt Ministry of Tourism and Antiquities.

Traveller must hold a valid Canadian visa that has been used at least once. Visa must be unexpired at time of travel to Egypt. Excluded nationalities: Afghanistan, Ethiopia, Iran, Iraq, Israel, Mali, Niger, Sudan, Yemen. Decree effective from 15 December 2020 as announced by Egypt Ministry of Tourism and Antiquities.

Traveller must hold a valid Australian visa that has been used at least once. Visa must be unexpired at time of travel to Egypt. Excluded nationalities: Afghanistan, Ethiopia, Iran, Iraq, Israel, Mali, Niger, Sudan, Yemen. Decree effective from 15 December 2020 as announced by Egypt Ministry of Tourism and Antiquities.

Traveller must hold a valid Japanese visa that has been used at least once. Visa must be unexpired at time of travel to Egypt. Excluded nationalities: Afghanistan, Ethiopia, Iran, Iraq, Israel, Mali, Niger, Sudan, Yemen. Decree effective from 15 December 2020 as announced by Egypt Ministry of Tourism and Antiquities.

Traveller must hold a valid New Zealand visa that has been used at least once. Visa must be unexpired at time of travel to Egypt. Excluded nationalities: Afghanistan, Ethiopia, Iran, Iraq, Israel, Mali, Niger, Sudan, Yemen. Decree effective from 15 December 2020 as announced by Egypt Ministry of Tourism and Antiquities.

Traveller must hold a valid US Green Card (Permanent Resident Card) valid for at least 6 months from arrival date. Excluded nationalities: Afghanistan, Ethiopia, Iran, Iraq, Israel, Mali, Niger, Sudan, Yemen.

credential.subtype: BRP or Share Code (UK Residence Permit)

conditional_access.conditions: Traveller must hold a valid UK Residence Permit (BRP or Share Code) valid for at least 6 months from date of visa issuance. This requirement applies to nationalities that are not themselves on the visa-on-arrival list (i.e. non-EU, non-USA, non-Australian, non-New Zealand nationals applying from the UK). For nationalities requiring pre-clearance (Afghanistan, Ethiopia, Iran, Mali, Niger, Palestine, Israel), the UK residence permit must still be presented with the clearance application.

Traveller must hold a valid Schengen area residence permit valid for at least 6 months from arrival date. Confirmed by Timatic and multiple tour-operator sources citing the December 2020 decree. Excluded nationalities: Afghanistan, Ethiopia, Iran, Iraq, Israel, Mali, Niger, Sudan, Yemen.

Traveller must hold a valid Canadian permanent resident card valid for at least 6 months from arrival date. Excluded nationalities: Afghanistan, Ethiopia, Iran, Iraq, Israel, Mali, Niger, Sudan, Yemen.
Traveller must hold a valid Australian residence permit valid for at least 6 months from arrival date. Excluded nationalities: Afghanistan, Ethiopia, Iran, Iraq, Israel, Mali, Niger, Sudan, Yemen.
Traveller must hold a valid Japanese residence permit valid for at least 6 months from arrival date. Excluded nationalities: Afghanistan, Ethiopia, Iran, Iraq, Israel, Mali, Niger, Sudan, Yemen.

credential.subtype: any valid New Zealand residence permit

conditional_access.conditions: Traveller must hold a valid New Zealand residence permit valid for at least 6 months from arrival date. Excluded nationalities: Afghanistan, Ethiopia, Iran, Iraq, Israel, Mali, Niger, Sudan, Yemen.

Agreement between Egypt and Saudi Arabia on mutual exemption from short-stay visas for holders of diplomatic and special passports. Signed 19 March 2026 by Egyptian Foreign Minister Badr Abdelatty and Saudi Foreign Minister Prince Faisal bin Farhan in Riyadh. Maximum short stay not specified in the official announcement.

Singapore-Egypt Agreement on Mutual Visa Exemption for Holders of Diplomatic Passports, effective 1 June 2024. Holders may enter, exit, and transit through Egypt without a visa for up to 90 days from date of entry for official work purposes.

cbi.program_name: Unit for Granting Egyptian Citizenship in Exchange for Investment

options.notes: Direct revenue (grant) of USD 250,000 deposited by bank transfer from abroad to Public Treasury Authority. Non-refundable. Source: official GAFI PDF at investinegypt.gov.eg.

Purchase of real estate owned by the State or public legal persons, minimum USD 500,000 transferred from abroad. If disposed of before 5 years from purchase date, USD 250,000 must be deposited as direct revenue to Public Treasury Authority. Non-refundable. Source: official GAFI PDF at investinegypt.gov.eg.

Setting up or participating in an investment project with at least USD 400,000 transferred from abroad and at least 40% participation of project capital. Per Investment Law No. 72 of 2017. If liquidated/suspended/disposed before 5 years of operation, USD 250,000 must be deposited as direct revenue. Source: official GAFI PDF at investinegypt.gov.eg.

Cash deposit of USD 1,000,000 at the Central Bank of Egypt by bank transfer from abroad. Refundable after 3 years in Egyptian pounds at CBE exchange rate on refund date (not exceeding exchange rate on deposit date), without interest. Source: official GAFI PDF at investinegypt.gov.eg.

Cash deposit of USD 750,000 at the Central Bank of Egypt by bank transfer from abroad. Refundable after 5 years in Egyptian pounds at CBE exchange rate on refund date (not exceeding exchange rate on deposit date), without interest. Source: official GAFI PDF at investinegypt.gov.eg.

cbi.notes: No residency period required prior to citizenship grant. Administrative fee of USD 10,000 transferred from abroad to the Central Bank of Egypt. Upon security authority approval, investor is granted temporary residence for 6 months to execute the chosen investment program. Citizenship granted to investor and minor children up to age 21, by decision of the Prime Minister. Unit is affiliated with the Prime Minister's Office and located at GAFI headquarters. Security clearance required.

programs.name: Residence for Foreign Investors (Investment Law No. 72 of 2017)
programs.notes: Article of Egypt's Investment Law No. 72 of 2017 states: 'The State shall grant the non-Egyptian investors residence in the Arab Republic of Egypt throughout the Project's terms without prejudice to the provisions of the relevant regulating laws and in the manner stipulated by the Executive Regulations of this Law.' Specific minimum investment thresholds and terms are set in executive regulations not reproduced on the official page. No specific dollar amounts could be confirmed from this official source.

validation_notes: CORRECTION: e-visa max_stay_days field updated from null to 30. The e-visa grants a stay of up to 30 days per entry. Single-entry e-visa is valid for 90 days from issue date; multiple-entry is valid 180 days from issue date. These validity windows were previously not recorded — only the 30-day max stay is the correct max_stay_days value.

CORRECTION: e-visa fees updated. As of April 2026, Egypt's official visa2egypt.gov.eg portal lists single-entry tourist e-visa at $30 and multiple-entry at $65 — both increased by $5 from prior $25/$60 rates. The prior record already had $30/$65 in notes but did not specify the fee change date. Notes now reference April 2026 fee update explicitly.

CORRECTION: Visa on arrival fee increased. Single-entry visa on arrival fee increased from $25 to $30 effective 1 March 2026 per visasnews.com reporting official airport sticker surcharge. Updated in VoA entry notes.

CORRECTION: Two new VoA nationalities added: Serbia (SRB) and Ukraine (UKR). Both confirmed by Egypt Consulate UK FAQ page (January 2026) and multiple corroborating aggregator sources. These were absent from the prior record.
CORRECTION: Russia added to VoA list. Egypt Consulate UK FAQ page lists Russian Federation as eligible for visa on arrival. Previously only listed in e_visa section.
CORRECTION: 'Macedonia' renamed to 'North Macedonia' in e_visa list to use correct current country name (consistent with VoA entry).
VERIFIED: Sinai resort stamp duration of 15 days is used per the egypt consulate UK visas page. The FAQ page of the same consulate states 14 days — this discrepancy was noted in entry notes.

VERIFIED: Conditional access entries (credential-based VoA for holders of US/UK/Schengen/Canada/Australia/Japan/New Zealand visas/permits) remain unchanged — no official Egyptian government .gov.eg primary URL found for the December 2020 decree but multiple embassy sources corroborate it.
VERIFIED: CBI program details unchanged — GAFI official URL confirmed still active.
VERIFIED: Diplomatic passport exemptions for SAU (sis.gov.eg, March 2026) and SGP (mfa.gov.sg, June 2024) remain confirmed.
gaps: Credential-based VoA rule (December 2020 decree): The rule allowing holders of valid and used visas from US/UK/Schengen/Canada/Australia/Japan/New Zealand to obtain visa on arrival was announced by Egypt's Ministry of Tourism and Antiquities on 15-17 December 2020. No permanent official Egyptian government .gov.eg URL publishing this rule was found; recorded under conditional_access based on multiple corroborating embassy and aggregator sources.

Diplomatic passport exemption list — comprehensive: Only two bilateral agreements were confirmed from official sources (Egypt-Saudi Arabia from sis.gov.eg dated 19 March 2026; Singapore-Egypt from mfa.gov.sg dated 1 June 2024). Many other countries (Greece, India, Brazil, Austria, Philippines, etc.) may have diplomatic/official/service passport exemptions per Timatic/Wikipedia but no official Egyptian government page listing all such agreements was accessible (mofa.gov.eg DNS was not resolvable).

Malaysia visa-free status: Egypt Consulate UK FAQ page states Malaysian nationals can stay up to 2 weeks without a visa. However, Malaysia is also listed on the official e-visa portal (visa2egypt.gov.eg). No official .gov.eg page was found confirming Malaysia's visa-free status definitively. Malaysia is retained in e_visa list pending official clarification; the reported visa-free status (14 days) should be verified against an official Egyptian government source.

GCC nationals visa-free entry: Multiple aggregator sources state GCC nationals (Bahrain, Kuwait, Oman, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, UAE) may enter Egypt visa-free for up to 90 days, and GCC residents of any nationality may obtain VoA. No official Egyptian government page was found confirming this specific rule. Not recorded until confirmed from an official .gov.eg or equivalent source.

Max stay days for e-Visa: The official visa2egypt.gov.eg FAQ page does not explicitly state the 30-day maximum stay in the retrieved text — this figure comes from corroborating travel sources and search result synthesis. Visa validity periods (90/180 days) are confirmed; the 30-day per-entry limit should be verified directly on the portal.

VoA multiple-entry fee: As of March 2026 the VoA single-entry fee increased to $30. The multiple-entry VoA fee was reported as still $60 as of that date but may have subsequently changed. Not updated pending official confirmation.
OCI (Overseas Citizen of India) cards: No evidence found from any official source that OCI cardholders receive any special entry treatment to Egypt beyond what Indian passport holders receive.

Residence by Investment (RBI) specific program details: Non-official sources cite a residence-by-investment scheme starting at USD 50,000 under a 2020 regulation, but no official government page confirming current RBI program terms (amounts, duration, conditions) was found.
Fast-track / digital nomad / talent visa schemes: No official Egyptian government page found confirming any such program.
CBI dual citizenship policy: Not stated on the official GAFI page.

Freedom-of-movement agreements: Egypt is a member of the Arab League but no official published list of visa-free Arab country nationals (for full Egypt-wide access) was found on official Egyptian immigration portals checked.
Saudi Arabia-Egypt diplomatic agreement (signed 19 March 2026): The maximum stay duration under the short-stay exemption was not specified in the official SIS announcement.
The Egypt Ministry of Foreign Affairs website (mofa.gov.eg) was not resolvable via DNS, preventing direct access to the official visa exemption treaty list.
